talk_metadata.institution_or_pharma: Brigham and Women's Hospital / Dana-Farber Cancer Institute
talk_metadata.talk_title: Agentic AI for Clinical Development in Oncology
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "We tend to take a fairly blunt approach to identifying cohorts for clinical trials... This leads to diluted signal and limited information to drive go/no-go decisions."
Speaker said: "We are not leading to decrements in accuracy or data quality, and actually we're improving the consistency, so reducing noise in adverse event determinations."
Speaker said: "We don't know if performance in these multi-choice question answer settings really translates to similar performance in real‑world applications."

trial_dilution_risk.target_biology: Clinical trial patient selection based on blunt, non‑molecular criteria
trial_dilution_risk.flawed_enrollment_criteria: Enrolling all‑comers without molecular or real‑world evidence stratification, leading to diluted signal
trial_dilution_risk.missing_biomarker: Molecular or real‑world predictive biomarker to enrich for responders
trial_dilution_risk.statistical_concern: Potential under‑powering due to signal dilution

key_data_gaps: No clinical trial efficacy data presented

crispro_opportunity.description: Need for molecular or real‑world evidence biomarkers to replace blunt patient selection and improve signal‑to‑noise in trials.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "If we're able to more precisely select patients, we can potentially run a clinical trial with fewer patients due to improved signal-to-noise ratio."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can generate and validate predictive biomarker signatures from multi‑modal data to enable molecularly stratified enrollment.

crispro_opportunity.description: Incorporate agentic AI‑driven real‑world evidence generation to inform eligibility criteria and comparator selection.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "Agentic AI can be used to develop the evidence base to guide clinical development decisions... what patient stratification options can be used, what endpoints should be selected."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO’s workflow engine can simulate trial designs with AI‑derived eligibility criteria and predict statistical power.

unresolved_questions: How can regulatory bodies evaluate and certify agentic AI systems that generate trial design recommendations?
What standards are needed for benchmarking real‑world performance of AI‑extracted adverse event data?

talk_metadata.talk_title: AI Agents in Clinical Tissue Medicine and Research
rhetorical_signals: Speaker said: "If we don't have high-quality data integration, standardization, harmonization, that conveyor belt approach is going to fall apart really rapidly."
Speaker said: "We have to be aware of attack vectors that could be used to manipulate the system... incidental prompt injection attacks can highly influence a model's decision."
Speaker said: "We are not an autonomous system; the pathologist can always intervene via the reasoning trace."

vulnerability_identified.failing_compound_or_target: AI pathology agents lacking robustness to prompt injection attacks
vulnerability_identified.mechanistic_blindspot: Vulnerability to malicious text prompts that overwrite visual diagnostic cues

competitive_moat_weakness.vulnerable_ip_or_chemistry: Open‑source pathology foundation models with limited proprietary protection
competitive_moat_weakness.ip_vulnerability_note: Speaker disclosed that the system is built on open‑source models that can be run on‑premise, indicating limited IP moat.
competitive_moat_weakness.clinical_strategy_weakness: Reliance on modular open tools may lead to inconsistent performance across institutions without standardized validation.

cognitive_dissonance: Speaker showed that the AI agent can generate a microscopic description and then pass it to an LLM for diagnosis, but later emphasized that "we have to be aware of attack vectors" that could completely flip the diagnosis, contradicting the confidence in the autonomous pipeline.

key_data_gaps: No prospective clinical validation of the end‑to‑end agentic pathology workflow
No quantitative performance metrics beyond prototype examples

crispro_opportunity.description: Develop robust detection and mitigation of prompt‑injection attacks in pathology AI agents.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "If you include a text statement that the slide looks completely healthy, the model will completely forget..."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO can embed adversarial‑training and provenance tracing to safeguard against malicious prompt manipulation.

crispro_opportunity.description: Leverage CrisPRO’s modular tool‑integration framework to provide standardized, auditable pipelines for multi‑modal pathology AI agents.
crispro_opportunity.transcript_evidence: Speaker said: "We have a reasoning trace for quality management purposes... you can get the reasoning of each of the single agents as well."
crispro_opportunity.crispro_angle: CrisPRO offers end‑to‑end traceability and versioned tool registries, addressing the need for governance highlighted by the speaker.

cited_competitors.name: TissueLab
cited_competitors.context: Mentioned as an example of existing pathology AI tools

cited_competitors.name: PathChat+ / SlideZEEK
cited_competitors.context: Cited as prior work using agentic workflows for diagnosis

unresolved_questions: How can standardized benchmarking be established for multi‑modal agentic pathology systems?
What governance frameworks are needed to certify safety against prompt‑injection attacks in clinical use?
